title: IBM API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: IBM Cloud API key detected. IBM Cloud API keys are 44-character credentials that provide authenticated access to IBM Cloud services and resources. These keys can have the same access as the user or service ID they are associated with, potentially granting access to resources across multiple IBM Cloud accounts. Exposing this key allows unauthorized parties to access cloud resources, manipulate services, incur billing charges, and access sensitive data stored in IBM Cloud services.
